name: Cam's Kiosk
cuisine: Cafe by day, wine bar / European share plates by night
description: Located in a wing of Abbotsford Convent, 1 St Heliers Street

name: Urbanstead
description: Minimum-waste sourdough bakery by Tivoli Road founders; opened Nov 2025, 203 Langridge St; filter/pour-over coffee only

name: Andrew's Hamburgers
description: Melbourne institution since 1939 at 144 Bridport St; former Franklin St CBD location now closed

name: Bistro Marigold
description: Pop-up by chef Rosheen Kaul (ex-Etta), took over Zia Rina's Cucina / Rena's in Armadale. Roving pop-up (also seen at Little Rose, Fitzroy).

name: Piccolo Vicolo
description: 5a North Street, Ascot Vale; breakfast & lunch venue known for viral meatball sandwich

name: The Mazu Cafe
description: 469 Whitehorse Rd, Balwyn; Taiwanese-influenced all-day breakfast and fusion dishes

name: Chaozhou Malaya Bistro Cafe
description: 70 Doncaster Rd; small family-run venue, bookings essential, closed Mon-Tue. Known for oyster omelette and braised duck

name: Hong's Dumplings
description: 872 Canterbury Rd; open Fri-Sun only, walk-ins/takeaway. Featured in The Age Good Food.

name: Chooka's
description: 1 Ballarat St; Studio Ghibli-themed hidden cafe specialising in rice balls; despite 'coffee' handle it is primarily a Japanese cafe

name: Cumbé
description: Mexico City-style tortas at 551 Sydney Rd, Brunswick; third Cumbé location after Mexico City.

name: Good Boys Deli
description: Sandwich/deli at 3 Ovens St; known for wagyu pastrami and focaccia subs

name: Lunar by Hikari
description: Sequel to CBD cafe Hikari; at 458 Sydney Road. Japanese pour-over coffee plus dishes like unagi rice and wagyu sukiyaki.

name: Alex Bake 3057
description: Factory 1, 10-12 Moreland Rd, Brunswick East 3057

name: Charrd
description: Take-out window operating out of Yakamoz; halal patties; open Thu-Sun

name: Daphne
cuisine: Public house / casual bar & diner (modern Australian)
description: 52-54 Lygon St (former Bar Romantica); from the Etta team (Hannah Green), chef Diana Desensi; opened Nov 2025; wines on tap, pubby classics, pasta/steak nights

name: Old Palm Liquor
cuisine: Modern South African-inspired wine bar & restaurant
description: 133B Lygon St; second venue from the Neighbourhood Wine team (Simon Denman & Almay Jordaan); fire-cooked food, extensive wine list

name: Sani
description: 63 Lygon Street; globally inspired all-day brunch cafe in a restored Victorian-era bank

name: A Little Joy
cuisine: Shanghai-style Chinese (dumplings, pan-fried buns, soy milk)
description: Chinese name 勿相忘. Multiple locations (Bourke St/Chinatown CBD, La Trobe St, Doncaster)

name: Antara
cuisine: Modern Australian restaurant, cafe & bakery (wood/coal fire)
description: 128 Exhibition St; a Windsor Group venue (Aru, Sunda, Parcs). Search indicates it has ceased trading at 128 Exhibition St, with the operator stating it will return in a different iteration.

name: Attakai Garden
description: CBD takeaway matcha window at 260 Collins St (Collins Arcade); same Attakai brand as Carlton

name: Aung Lo
description: Not Burmese; it is Thai-Japanese BBQ/hotpot in a hidden laneway (Flanigan Lane). From the team behind Pick Prik, Heng & Lang Baan. Handle matches Instagram.

name: BKK

name: Bakemono Bakers
description: At 273 Little Lonsdale St (laneway off it); known for croissants and pastries, often sold out early

name: Bing Chillin

description: Multiple locations: CBD, Doncaster East, Clayton, Glen Waverley. Input area was just 'Melbourne'; CBD is the flagship/city store.

name: Bloomwood Coffee & Food
description: G01/121 Exhibition St; opened 2024, Japandi-influenced. Second location opened at Bourke Street's Galleria.

name: Boon Choou
cuisine: Thai (regional - central, northern Lanna, north-eastern Isan, southern Pattani)
description: 11 Heffernan Lane, Melbourne CBD. Official spelling often rendered 'Boonchoou'. Opened by ex-Chat Thai (Sydney) owners in the former Niubi/Wing Ching site.

name: Bowltiful Halal Lanzhou Beef Noodle
description: Multiple locations (Elizabeth St, Swanston St CBD, plus Glen Waverley and Box Hill)

name: Butchers Diner
description: 10 Bourke St; bar serving Australian meat, burgers, steaks

name: Caffe E Torta
description: 314 Little Collins St; also has a Royal Arcade location

name: Caretaker's Cottage
description: Tiny bluestone cottage cocktail bar at 139-141 Little Lonsdale St, opened 2022; World's 50 Best Bars listed; known for martini and Guinness boilermaker; walk-ins only

name: Common Cuts
description: 380 Russell St; opened 2025 by Clove Group, ex-Kisume chef Reki Reinantha

name: Commonplace Coffee Brewers
description: Official name is 'Commonplace' (one word). At 104T/28 Rose Lane, Melbourne 3000. Serves Toby's Estate single origins, batch brew & V60.

name: Coppe Pan Japanese Bakery
description: Flagship in Melbourne Central, LG07/211 La Trobe St

name: Crystal Jade Restaurant
description: Level 1, 154 Little Bourke St, Chinatown; modern Cantonese with daily yum cha, signature wok-fried snow crab in foie gras

name: Dongdaemoon
description: Also styled 'Dong Dae Moon' / 'Dongdaemun'; 466 Little Lonsdale St in Melbourne's Koreatown

name: EARL Canteen

name: FOODAO
description: 211 La Trobe St; also a Melbourne Central location

name: Fishbowl
description: National chain, 50+ locations across Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane & Gold Coast; Instagram handle matches @fishbowl_fuel

name: French Fix

description: Japanese shaved-ice dessert shop at 28 Guildford Lane, Melbourne CBD 3000

name: Gimbap Jang Go Mama
description: 147 Lonsdale Street; tiny 6-seat eatery, dine-in and takeaway. Official listing name is 'Gimbap Jang Go Mama'.

name: Göz City
description: Originally CBD (Little Collins St / Madame Brussels Lane); also a Port Melbourne location

name: Hansik
description: At 51 A'Beckett St; also known as Hansik Melbourne Korean Restaurant & Bar; website hansikdang.com.au

name: Heng (Heng Thai Local Food Hall)

description: Authentic Northern Chinese restaurant specialising in tableside-carved Peking duck, with daily lunchtime yum cha. Located at 1/137 A'Beckett St, Melbourne CBD. Official site hualupekingduck.com.au.

description: Halal-certified; multiple locations (York St, Barangaroo/International Towers, George St)

name: Kombu

name: Lang Baan
description: 1 Rose Lane, Melbourne CBD; handle taken from Instagram @langbaan.melb (not in input).

name: Little Ramen Bar
description: 369 Little Collins St

name: Maison Bâtard

name: Mankeumi

name: Overlay Coffee
description: Located at 320 Little Lonsdale St; known for peanut butter cream latte. Instagram on listings appears as overlaycoffee.com.au

name: Pa Tong Thai

name: Regale

name: Pacific Seafood BBQ House
description: Input "Pacific House" matches Pacific Seafood BBQ House (also listed as Pacific House Restaurant) at 240 Victoria St, Richmond; long-standing Cantonese roast/BBQ institution, BYO

name: Warkop
description: Multiple locations: original 15-seat cafe in Richmond (12 Risley St) plus a CBD location on Little Collins St

name: Attica
description: 74 Glen Eira Rd; chef Ben Shewry; World's 50 Best, featured on Netflix Chef's Table

name: Aoi Tsuki
description: 384 Punt Rd; 12-seat omakase counter (input area 'Melbourne' corrected to South Yarra)

name: Emotional Oranges
description: 367 Chapel St, South Yarra (near Prahran Market). Specialises in layered crepe cakes.

name: France-Soir
description: 11-13 Toorak Rd; open since 1986

name: Steer Dining Room
description: New York-style steakhouse specialising in Australian and Japanese Wagyu; official Kobe A5 licensed restaurant

name: Warung Coffee & Sandwiches
description: 667 Chapel Street, South Yarra; opened ~2024 by barista Ryan Dewantara; cafe meets Asian grocer

name: itteki 一滴 Matcha + Cafe
description: Multiple locations (South Yarra, Southbank, Toorak); handle is the South Yarra flagship

name: Kolkata Cricket Club
description: At Crown Melbourne, Level 1/8 Whiteman St, Southbank. Led by chef Mischa Tropp. Opened 2025.

name: Bun Bun Bakery Springvale
description: 288 Springvale Rd, Shop 1; award-winning banh mi, takeaway only, cash/PayID only (no cards)

name: Martabak Pecenongan 78
cuisine: Indonesian martabak (sweet/savoury stuffed pancakes) & noodles; halal
description: At 38 Buckingham Ave, co-located with Rojo Sambel as 'Rojo Sambel & Martabak Pecenongan 78'. Australian outpost of the famous Indonesian martabak chain (founded 1960, 400+ outlets in Indonesia). Serves martabak pancakes plus Indonesian street noodles; halal.

name: Rojo Sambel & Martabak Pecenongan 78
description: At 38 Buckingham Ave. Co-located with Martabak Pecenongan 78 as a single venue. Indonesian comfort food with hand-crushed sambals; counter service, halal (no pork/alcohol). Linked to Indonesian celebrity Raffi Ahmad.

name: Cô Thư Quán St Albans
description: 320 Main Rd E, St Albans. Part of a multi-location Melbourne group (Richmond, Springvale, Abbotsford); the handle is the group-wide Instagram, not St Albans-specific.

name: Garam Merica
description: 348 St Kilda Rd (Seasons Botanic Garden); some listings tag it CBD but it's on St Kilda Rd, City of Port Phillip

name: Pizzicotto Pizzeria e Cucina
description: 68/70 Woodland St; Sicilian-led, established 2018, dinner Tue-Sun

name: Café Sunshine & Salamatea
cuisine: Persian / Middle Eastern cafe & restaurant (brunch by day, banquets by night)
description: 18A Market Rd, Sunshine; refugee-run social enterprise; sister venue to Kababi; chef Hamed Allahyari

name: Federal Taung Zalat
description: Home-style Burmese hole-in-the-wall inside Sunshine Plaza food court.

name: Kababi
description: 18 Market Rd, Sunshine; sister venue to Salamatea; chef Hamed Allahyari

name: Xuan Banh Cuon
description: Family-run; famous for steamed rice noodle rolls; 232 Hampshire Road

name: Yuki Tei Tempura Bar
description: Shop 11, 1a Grange Rd, behind Toorak Village. Omakase tempura bar for dinner, tight lunch menu (katsu don, curry, gyoza).

name: 279 | Coffee & Musubi
description: 279 Victoria St; corner cafe near Queen Vic Market (also referenced as North Melbourne)

name: Chàm
cuisine: Modern Vietnamese cafe & brunch (banh mi croissant, pho, egg coffee)
description: 25/31 Peel Street, opposite Queen Victoria Market

name: JUSIK
description: Shop 1/39-47 Peel St, West Melbourne; input area was blank, resolved via search

name: Myth Cafe
description: Short for 'My Taste of Home'; Malaysian Hakka hawker breakfast/lunch at 48 Rosslyn St

name: Sanhe Congee
description: 185 Victoria St, West Melbourne, near Queen Victoria Market. ~30 congee varieties; moved from takeaway-only to a larger shopfront.

name: The Flour Melbourne
description: Artisan patisserie/cafe at 199 Victoria St, West Melbourne; known for Mont Blanc, canele and award-winning coffee program

name: Warung Agus
description: Family-run Balinese restaurant operating since 1989; signatures include babi guling, ayam betutu, nasi campur. Handle matches Instagram @warungagus.

name: Na He Bistro
description: 147 Chapel St; menu inspired by ethnic minority cuisines of Yunnan Province

name: Coracle
description: Minimalist cafe blending Vietnamese, Japanese & Hong Kong dishes; 63-65 Anderson St; input name 'Coracle Dining' shortens to 'Coracle'
